result.markdown:
--- title: "Protein Motif Analysis" subtitle: "Eukaryotic Linear Motifs in Potyviridae CI helicase — gget/ELM search and genus-specificity" author: "chrissi m." date: last-modified format: html: theme: cosmo toc: true toc-depth: 3 toc-location: left code-fold: true code-tools: true page-layout: full execute: echo: true warning: false message: false error: true --- ## Overview The cylindrical inclusion (CI) helicase carries a short conserved region that, across Potyviridae, overlaps a number of **eukaryotic linear motifs (ELMs)** — short functional peptide motifs catalogued in the ELM database [@elm2024]. Here I extract the CI motif-flanking region from each isolate in the ICTV Potyviridae alignment [@inouenagata2022], search it against ELM, and ask which motifs are **characteristic of particular genera**. The ELM search is run with the [`gget`](https://github.com/pachterlab/gget) Python package (`gget elm`), which reproduces the ELM webserver's two search modes: an ortholog (DIAMOND) search against ELM-annotated proteins and a direct regex search. It runs in a self-contained `pixi` environment inside WSL; the per-isolate results are written to `data/elm_gget/` by `scripts/prep_elm_regions.R` → `scripts/gget_elm_run.py` → `scripts/viz_elm_motifs.R`. This chapter reads those results. ## CI motif region I anchor the CI region on the alignment consensus at the conserved `VASYN` motif (allowing one mismatch) and take a ±20 aa window. The alignment is read from the **local** ICTV file (`data/OPSR.Poty.Fig3.v16_align.txt`). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} protaln <- readAAStringSet(file.path(data_dir, "OPSR.Poty.Fig3.v16_align.txt"), format = "fasta") cons <- consensusString(protaln, ambiguityMap = "X") m <- matchPattern("VASYN", cons, max.mismatch = 1) anchora <- start(m)[1] anchorb <- end(m)[1] region <- subseq(protaln, anchora - 20, anchorb + 20) # aligned window cat(sprintf("VASYN anchor at consensus %d-%d; region columns %d-%d (%d isolates)\n", anchora, anchorb, anchora - 20, anchorb + 20, length(region))) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` VASYN anchor at consensus 3698-3702; region columns 3678-3722 (208 isolates) ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} region ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` AAStringSet object of length 208: width seq names [1] 45 ERVQT---YAK---GGCIVFCSTQKETEESAKALKA---SGVIAL CeLV_MH932227 [2] 45 GDLLD---ATKH--DSVLVFLKGRNECVKAAHAWNSAVIGEKKAF OMV_AJ306719_AJ30... [3] 45 GDLLD---ISKH--DTALVFLAGRPECIKAANAWNASVTGEKRAF BaYMV_AJ132269_AJ... [4] 45 GDILD---LTRH--DTVLVFLSGRPECVKAATLWNASITGDKRAF WSSMV_MH645042_MH... [5] 45 DDLLD---VTKY--DTVLVFLAGRPECVKAANAWNASITGEKRAF WYMV_D86635_D86634 ... ... ... [204] 45 GANSD---VLAKG-SNILVYVASYNEVDTLSKMLTD---KAYRVT MVCV_MZ555807 [205] 45 GSNID---VISKG-DNILVYVASYNEVDTLSRGLLD---KGFKVT PKgMV_ON428226 [206] 45 GVNYD---VITKG-DNILVYVASYNEVDMLSKLLLE---KGHKVT JVT_KT222674 [207] 45 GCNSD---ILKHG-VNVLVYVASYNEVDTLSKLLTD---RSFKVS PPV_D13751 [208] 45 GANCD---ITSYG-HNILVYVSSYNEVDSLSKMLLD---KSYKVT RanMMV_OL742438 ``` ::: ::: ## Isolate metadata and genus assignment Each sequence name encodes a virus abbreviation and one or more accessions. Genus is assigned by joining to the ICTV VMR table [@ictvvmr2024] — first by GENBANK accession, then, for isolates that are not VMR species exemplars, by matching the virus abbreviation to the VMR *"Virus name abbreviation(s)"* column. This two-stage join (implemented in `scripts/prep_elm_regions.R`) leaves **no** isolate unclassified. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} meta <- readr::read_tsv(file.path(elm_dir, "region_meta.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) meta %>% count(genus, genus_source, name = "n_isolates") %>% arrange(desc(n_isolates)) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Isolates per genus and how genus was assigned.") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Isolates per genus and how genus was assigned. |genus |genus_source | n_isolates| |:------------|:------------|----------:| |Potyvirus |accession | 145| |Potyvirus |abbreviation | 21| |Bymovirus |accession | 6| |Ipomovirus |accession | 6| |Macluravirus |accession | 6| |Tritimovirus |accession | 6| |Poacevirus |accession | 3| |Rymovirus |accession | 3| |Arepavirus |accession | 2| |Macluravirus |abbreviation | 2| |Phragmivirus |accession | 2| |Roymovirus |accession | 2| |Bevemovirus |accession | 1| |Brambyvirus |accession | 1| |Celavirus |accession | 1| |Ipomovirus |abbreviation | 1| ::: ::: ## ELM search results (gget) `gget elm` was run per isolate in ortholog + regex mode. As expected for viral CI proteins, the **ortholog** search returns nothing (ELM's ortholog set is eukaryotic), so the **regex** motif matches carry the signal — the same content the ELM webserver returns for a direct sequence query. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} regex_path <- file.path(elm_dir, "elm_regex.tsv") have_gget <- file.exists(regex_path) if (have_gget) { elm <- readr::read_tsv(regex_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>% dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(meta, query = name, virus_abbr, genus), by = "query") cat(sprintf("%d motif hits across %d isolates and %d ELM motif classes\n", nrow(elm), dplyr::n_distinct(elm$query), dplyr::n_distinct(elm$ELMIdentifier))) elm %>% dplyr::count(ELMType, name = "hits") %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(hits)) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "ELM hits by motif type.") } else { message("data/elm_gget/elm_regex.tsv not found. ", "Regenerate with scripts/prep_elm_regions.R -> gget_elm_run.py.") } ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 3989 motif hits across 208 isolates and 96 ELM motif classes ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: ELM hits by motif type. |ELMType | hits| |:-------|----:| |LIG | 2209| |MOD | 685| |TRG | 536| |DOC | 258| |CLV | 212| |NA | 76| |DEG | 13| ::: ::: ### What the motifs are — reference table Throughout this chapter motifs are labelled by their **ELM identifier** (e.g. `LIG_SH2_STAT5`), which is compact but opaque. The table below unpacks every detected class: its **functional-site name**, ELM **type** (CLV cleavage, DEG degron, DOC docking, LIG ligand-binding, MOD post-translational modification, TRG targeting), the **canonical regular-expression pattern** that defines the motif, a brief functional **description**, and the number of CI-region isolates carrying it. Sort by *n_isolates* for the near-universal motifs; filter by *Type* or search the description for a functional class of interest. Each identifier links to its ELM database entry. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} elm_ref <- elm %>% dplyr::group_by(ELMIdentifier) %>% dplyr::summarise( `Functional site` = dplyr::first(FunctionalSiteName), Type = dplyr::first(ELMType), `Canonical regex` = dplyr::first(Regex), Description = dplyr::first(Description), n_isolates = dplyr::n_distinct(query), .groups = "drop") %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n_isolates)) %>% dplyr::mutate(ELMIdentifier = sprintf( '%s', ELMIdentifier, ELMIdentifier)) DT::datatable( elm_ref, rownames = FALSE, escape = FALSE, filter = "top", options = list(pageLength = 12, scrollX = TRUE, columnDefs = list(list(width = "320px", targets = 4))), caption = paste("All ELM linear-motif classes detected in the CI motif-flanking", "region, with functional-site name, type, canonical regex,", "description and isolate count. Identifiers link to the ELM", "database.")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## Motif landscape across isolates Motif presence per isolate, ELM classes on **y**, isolate fragments on **x**, faceted and coloured by genus (each panel is one genus, width proportional to isolate count). The wide bands are motifs shared across essentially all *Potyvirus* isolates; the narrow genus panels reveal motifs restricted to particular lineages. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} pa <- elm %>% dplyr::distinct(query, genus, ELMIdentifier) genus_sizes <- meta %>% dplyr::count(genus, name = "n_iso") %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n_iso)) isolate_order <- meta %>% dplyr::mutate(genus = factor(genus, levels = genus_sizes$genus)) %>% dplyr::arrange(genus, virus_abbr) %>% dplyr::pull(name) pa <- pa %>% dplyr::mutate( query = factor(query, levels = isolate_order), ELMIdentifier = forcats::fct_rev(factor(ELMIdentifier)), genus = factor(genus, levels = genus_sizes$genus)) ggplot(pa, aes(query, ELMIdentifier, fill = genus)) + geom_tile() + facet_grid(cols = vars(genus), scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") + scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo", guide = "none") + labs(title = "ELM linear motifs across Potyviridae CI region fragments", x = "Isolate fragment (grouped by genus)", y = "ELM motif class") + theme_minimal(base_size = 8) + theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), panel.grid = element_blank(), panel.spacing.x = unit(1.5, "pt"), strip.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 0, size = 7), strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey95", colour = NA)) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](04_protein_motifs_files/figure-html/grid-1.png){width=1440} ::: ::: ## Genus-characteristic motifs Motifs ranked by how much their prevalence varies across genera (restricted to genera with ≥3 isolates). Because genus sizes are very unequal, each call carries a **confidence** based on the size of the genus driving it, and a **Wilson lower bound** on the prevalence — so a motif present in 3/3 isolates of a small genus (point prevalence 1.0, lower bound ≈ 0.44) is not overstated relative to one present in 166/166 *Potyvirus* isolates (lower bound ≈ 0.98). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} spec <- readr::read_tsv(file.path(elm_dir, "elm_motif_specificity.tsv"), show_col_types = FALSE) MIN_N <- 3; CONF_LOW <- 5; TOP_M <- 30 big_genera <- genus_sizes %>% dplyr::filter(n_iso >= MIN_N) %>% dplyr::pull(genus) top_motifs <- spec %>% dplyr::slice_max(spec_var, n = TOP_M) %>% dplyr::pull(ELMIdentifier) prev_long <- spec %>% dplyr::filter(ELMIdentifier %in% top_motifs) %>% dplyr::select(ELMIdentifier, dplyr::all_of(as.character(big_genera))) %>% tidyr::pivot_longer(-ELMIdentifier, names_to = "genus", values_to = "prevalence") lab_map <- genus_sizes %>% dplyr::filter(genus %in% big_genera) %>% dplyr::mutate(label = sprintf("%s (n=%d)%s", genus, n_iso, ifelse(n_iso < CONF_LOW, " †", ""))) lm <- setNames(lab_map$label, lab_map$genus) prev_long <- prev_long %>% dplyr::mutate( genus_lab = factor(lm[genus], levels = rev(lm[as.character(big_genera)])), ELMIdentifier = factor(ELMIdentifier, levels = spec$ELMIdentifier[spec$ELMIdentifier %in% top_motifs])) lowconf <- spec$ELMIdentifier[spec$confidence == "low"] prev_long <- prev_long %>% dplyr::mutate(lowconf = ELMIdentifier %in% lowconf & prevalence > 0.5) ggplot(prev_long, aes(ELMIdentifier, genus_lab, fill = prevalence)) + geom_tile(colour = "grey92") + geom_tile(data = dplyr::filter(prev_long, lowconf), colour = "#2b8cbe", linewidth = 0.7, fill = NA) + scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1, limits = c(0, 1), name = "Prevalence") + labs(title = "Genus-characteristic ELM motifs", caption = paste0("† genus with <", CONF_LOW, " isolates (low-confidence); blue-ringed cells: ", "characteristic call from a small genus."), x = "ELM motif class", y = "Genus") + theme_minimal(base_size = 9) + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7), plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 7, colour = "grey30"), panel.grid = element_blank()) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](04_protein_motifs_files/figure-html/spec-1.png){width=1152} ::: ::: The highest-confidence genus-characteristic calls (largest driving genus). These are the **data-selected "interesting" motifs** — chosen by an unbiased cross-genus prevalence-variance ranking rather than by hand — so each is shown with its functional-site name and canonical pattern: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # motif -> (name, regex) lookup from the regex hit table motif_meta <- elm %>% dplyr::group_by(ELMIdentifier) %>% dplyr::summarise(`Functional site` = dplyr::first(FunctionalSiteName), Regex = dplyr::first(Regex), .groups = "drop") spec %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(spec_var)) %>% dplyr::transmute(ELMIdentifier, top_genus, top_n, top_prev = round(top_prev, 2), wilson_lwr = round(top_prev_wilson_lwr, 2), confidence, spec_var = round(spec_var, 3)) %>% dplyr::slice_head(n = 15) %>% dplyr::left_join(motif_meta, by = "ELMIdentifier") %>% dplyr::relocate(`Functional site`, Regex, .after = ELMIdentifier) %>% knitr::kable(caption = paste("Top 15 motifs by cross-genus prevalence", "variance, with functional-site name, canonical", "regex and small-sample confidence.")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Top 15 motifs by cross-genus prevalence variance, with functional-site name, canonical regex and small-sample confidence. |ELMIdentifier |Functional site |Regex |top_genus | top_n| top_prev| wilson_lwr|confidence | spec_var| |:------------------|:------------------------------------------------|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|:------------|-----:|--------:|----------:|:----------|--------:| |DOC_PP1_RVXF_1 |PP1-docking motif RVXF |..[RK].{0,1}[VIL][^P][FW]. |Macluravirus | 8| 1.00| 0.68|moderate | 0.238| |LIG_SH2_NCK_1 |Phosphotyrosine ligands bound by SH2 domains |([^KR][^KR](Y)[DE][^GWFY][AI][SDENQTAGYFP])|([^KR][^KR](Y)[STNA][^GWFY][PV][SDENQTAGYFP])|(..Y[DE][^GWFY][PV][SDENQTAGYFP])|Rymovirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.223| |LIG_LIR_Nem_3 |Atg8 protein family ligands |[EDST].{0,2}[WFY]..[ILVFY] |Rymovirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.222| |TRG_ENDOCYTIC_2 |Y-based sorting signal |Y..[LMVIF] |Rymovirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.210| |LIG_SH2_STAT5 |Phosphotyrosine ligands bound by SH2 domains |(Y)[VLTFIC].. |Potyvirus | 166| 1.00| 0.98|high | 0.201| |LIG_SH2_SFK_2 |Phosphotyrosine ligands bound by SH2 domains |[^KR][^KR](Y)([DEVYI][^KRH][ILVF]|[DEVYI][^KRH][AQPMCW]|[NQSTAFL][^KRH][ILVF])((.{0,3}[DE])|.|$) |Rymovirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.200| |LIG_LIR_Gen_1 |Atg8 protein family ligands |[EDST].{0,2}[WFY][^RKPGWFY][^PG][ILVFM]((.{0,4}[PLAFIVMY])|($)|(.{0,3}[ED])) |Rymovirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.179| |MOD_Plk_4 |Polo-like kinase phosphosites |..[^IRFW]([ST])[ILMVFWY][ILMVFWY]. |Poacevirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.157| |MOD_CK2_1 |Casein kinase 2 (CK2) Phosphorylation site |...([ST])..E |Rymovirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.155| |MOD_NEK2_2 |NEK2 phosphorylation site |[FLMW][^P][^P]([ST])[^PDEGAN][RKH]. |Tritimovirus | 6| 1.00| 0.61|moderate | 0.148| |MOD_NEK2_1 |NEK2 phosphorylation site |[FLM][^PVIED][^PVID]([ST])[MLIVF][RKH]. |Tritimovirus | 6| 1.00| 0.61|moderate | 0.143| |LIG_Pex14_2 |Pex14 ligand motif |F...[WF] |Tritimovirus | 6| 1.00| 0.61|moderate | 0.139| |LIG_WRC_WIRS_1 |WAVE regulatory complex (WRC) binding site motif |[FYILMV].[TS]F(G|[^P]). |Tritimovirus | 6| 1.00| 0.61|moderate | 0.139| |MOD_CK1_1 |Casein kinase 1 (CK1) Phosphorylation site |S..([ST])... |Poacevirus | 3| 1.00| 0.44|low | 0.122| |TRG_Pf-PMV_PEXEL_1 |Apicomplexan export motif |(R.[LI].[EDQ])|(R.L..[EDQ])|(K.L.E) |Tritimovirus | 6| 0.83| 0.44|moderate | 0.112| ::: ::: ## Notes - The concatenated-submission workflow (`build_concat()` + junction-hit flagging) used for the original single ELM webserver query is retained in `potytools` but is no longer needed here, since `gget` lets us query each isolate directly. - The gget ELM database footprint is ~3 MB (ELM-curated instances/classes); the larger DIAMOND binary lives inside the disposable pixi env, not in the project.
